meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't validate ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't validate ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup Guide for Students: Validate Your SaaS Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn to validate your SaaS ideas using analytics. A beginner-friendly guide leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first portfolio project.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing if your app has value. By integrating lightweight analytics, you transform raw traffic into actionable data. Use Antigravity to instrument your tracking and Claude Code to automate the implementation. This loop validates demand early, preventing wasted months building features that nobody actually wants to use.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks hunched over your keyboard, crafting what you believe is a revolutionary interface. You hit deploy, share the link, and… silence. The void of a zero-user launch is the graveyard where most student projects go to die. It is not because your code is bad; it is because you are flying blind without a compass.

Data is the bridge between a hobbyist project and a professional SaaS. By implementing a robust analytics pipeline, you stop speculating about what your users need and start seeing exactly where they drop off. This guide will show you how to turn your application into a data-driven laboratory using modern AI-assisted tools.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves a user finds value in your app. For a student project, this is usually 'Account Created' or 'First AI Prompt Generated'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Manager. Help me define the 'North Star' metric for a SaaS app that helps students organize their study notes. List 3 key events I should track to validate user engagement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Propose a schema for a PostHog event tracking file for my study note app.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate a lightweight tracking SDK. We use this to capture user sessions without bloating the codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular TypeScript provider for PostHog analytics that can be initialized in my main App.tsx file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a tracking.ts utility file and integrate it into the main entry point.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Wrap your primary call-to-action buttons with event tracking functions to capture intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a reusable React hook useAnalytics that wraps the tracking library and handles event properties like user_id and timestamp.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update the Signup button to trigger a trackEvent call with the property source: landing_page.'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay
steps.description: Enable session recording to watch how users navigate your UI. This reveals usability friction you would never notice otherwise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain how to enable session recording in my analytics provider while respecting user privacy and excluding sensitive input fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the analytics provider to scrub sensitive inputs from session recordings.'

steps.title: Build a Validation Dashboard
steps.description: Create a simple view to visualize the data. If you see active usage, you have validated your hypothesis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a SQL query or dashboard configuration snippet that visualizes daily active users and conversion rates for my specific events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a README section explaining how to interpret the conversion funnel for my new app.'

pro_tips: Track 'rage clicks'—when a user clicks the same spot repeatedly, it's a signal that your UI is broken or confusing.
Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information). Use anonymous UUIDs to keep your project compliant and safe.
Focus on the 'Activation' event. If users don't perform the core action within the first 30 seconds, your onboarding is failing.
Use a 'feature flag' approach to toggle analytics off in development to keep your testing data clean.
Set up a Slack or Discord webhook to receive a notification every time a new user completes your 'North Star' action.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, share your link with 10 peers. Watch the dashboard for 48 hours. If you see users dropping off at a specific step, iterate on that UI component using Claude Code, then re-deploy.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student writing code; you are a builder analyzing human behavior. With these tools, you can prove your ideas work and build a portfolio that stands out to recruiters. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
